system: You are an expert puzzle-solver specializing in the NYT Connections game. Follow the rules strictly and provide structured responses.
user:
HOW TO PLAY 1. You are given 16 words that form 4 hidden groups of 4 related words each. 2. Sort all 16 words into the 4 groups they belong to. 3. This is ONE-SHOT: you submit ALL 4 groups in a SINGLE response. There is no feedback and no retry. 4. Every word must be used exactly once. Each group must contain EXACTLY 4 words.
TRAPS The puzzle may contain TRAP SETS: 4 (or more) clues that form a convincing category but do NOT form one of the real groups (locking such a set in would make the puzzle unsolvable). After your answer, pick the most likely trap set(s) — up to 2 — or state N/A. Not all puzzles have traps.
SCORING Base: 1 point per correctly matched group; matching all 4 earns 3 points. Because getting exactly 3 groups right forces the 4th, base scores are 0, 1, 2, or 3. Trap bonus (+2, all or nothing): - +2 if every trap set you claim is a genuine designed trap (claiming 1 correct trap is enough). - +2 if you state N/A and the puzzle truly has no traps. - ANY incorrect trap claim forfeits the entire bonus — do not guess wildly. Omitting the traps block scores no bonus. Maximum: 5 points per puzzle. If your answer is not in a valid format — not exactly 4 groups of 4 words, or the 16 words do not exactly match the puzzle's words — you score 0, including the trap bonus.
RESPONSE FORMAT: Provide your response in this exact structure:
[Your reasoning]
[Four lines, each with EXACTLY four words, ALL CAPS, comma-separated — one line per group]
[Up to two lines, each with FOUR OR MORE words, ALL CAPS, comma-separated — one line per trap set. Or a single line reading N/A if you believe there are no traps.]
[A number between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating your confidence in this answer]
